title: commidendrum rugosum
intro: Commidendrum rugosum, also known as the 'Rough-leaved Silver Tree', is a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It is native to the southwestern part of Australia, where it is often found in dry, sandy soils. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its leaves and stems exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The leaves can be made into a tea, and the plant's extracts have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and skin infections.
